evals.prompt: Add tool allowlists, rate limits, and append-only audit logs to this production coding agent that calls external APIs.
evals.expected_behavior: Chooses a governance level from risk, defines a policy with allowed tools and limits, enforces at the tool boundary, and wires audit events for allowed, denied, and error outcomes with fail-closed behavior.
assertions: Defines a governance policy with allowed tools or blocked patterns
Enforces controls at the tool boundary rather than only documenting them
Includes audit trail or logging of allowed and denied events
Mentions fail-closed behavior when governance checks error

evals.prompt: We have multiple agents that can call privileged tools. Add trust scoring and human approval for sensitive operations.
evals.expected_behavior: Adds multi-agent trust scoring before privileged access and requires human approval for sensitive tools without mixing in supply-chain integrity manifests.
assertions: Mentions trust scoring or multi-agent privilege gating
Mentions human approval for sensitive tool operations
Stays focused on runtime governance rather than package integrity hashing

evals.prompt: Generate a SHA-256 manifest for this plugin and gate promotion on integrity verification.
evals.expected_behavior: Routes away from agent-governance because the task is supply-chain integrity, not runtime tool policy.
assertions: Does not implement runtime tool allowlists for a manifest/integrity request
Routes toward agent-supply-chain or integrity-manifest work instead
